TextENGNew: As we chatted together, Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun came over and timidly spoke to us.

TextENGNew: ``...That man?``
At that, Coach lowered his voice just slightly, as if checking that no one was nearby.
``Ooishi.``
``......Ooishi. ......The man from earlier...``
The events of just now came rushing back.
The indescribable sense of foreboding I had felt from that man came rushing back.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ``...He's a little unhinged.``
``...The chain of incidents surrounding Oyashiro-sama's curse have all been resolved. And yet he alone refuses to accept that.``
......Refuses to accept it?
...The police had declared the cases solved, and yet a detective who belonged to that very same police... refused to accept it?
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``...People say that something unfortunate is sure to befall anyone he gets close to. ...In Hinamizawa, they call Ooishi Oyashiro-sama's—``
``Kei-chaaaaan!!``
The door flew open with a bang, and Mion came rushing in.
A beat later, Rena and Rika-chan arrived, followed by Tomita-kun, Okamura-kun, and several other classmates.
``K-Kei-chan...! Did Ooishi do something to you?! Are you hurt...?!``
``Calm down, Mion-san. He isn't injured. I only put a compress on just to be safe.``
``Keiichi-kun, are you really okay? Really?! Everyone was saying you were being strangled or something...!``
``Calm down, Rena. He just squeezed my shoulders really hard. Look—not even a bruise, right?``
``Honestly!! What were you all doing?! We're friends, aren't we?! Why were you just standing there?! Why didn't any of you help Kei-chan?!``
``W-We're sorry... Class President...``
Mion bared her teeth and turned on our classmates.
changeReason: Significantly different wording changes the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: Tomita-kun and the others hung their heads in silence.
``Knock it off, Mion... Tomita-kun and the others weren't even there. Stop blaming them.``
``Damn it...... Damn it all!! Ooishi...!!``
Mion kicked at the floor with her heel again and again, barely containing her fury.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Our teacher, drawn by the commotion, arrived as well.
``Hey, now, everyone! You can't make this kind of noise in the nurse's office! Class President—take everyone out, please!``
``Come on, everyone! Let's go! Keiichi-kun is all right.``
``You too, Mii-chan.``
With Mion's emotions running too high, Rena stepped in and quietly shepherded everyone out of the nurse's office...
The detective from Okinomiya Station, Kuraudo Ooishi...
...In the end, the first impression he'd made on me had been exactly right.
The vague unease I'd been carrying since yesterday had grown heavier with Satoko's absence, and Ooishi's appearance had turned it from unease into certainty.
``Well then. ...I'll take my leave here. I originally came to speak with Chie-sensei.``
``...Oh... you had business at the school to begin with... I'm sorry for taking up your time with something like this...``
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: "Don't be so formal about it," he said, with an easy smile.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ``If anything, I gained something from it—an unexpected perk, you might say. ...I was treated to quite a thorough look at your smooth, fine skin, Maebara-kun.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: With the typical (for Coach, at least) parting comment to send us off.

changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ``They are rumors, Maebara-san. ...Only rumors.``
What I had said wasn't funny—not because of any rumor about Ooishi being Oyashiro-sama's emissary, or about him making contact with that year's sacrifice in advance.
